steps.title: Mark false impression versus fact
steps.content: The first sentence says a viewer might assume a real hole had opened in the street. The next sentence states that Tanner created only an illusion of depth, so the transition must reverse that assumption.

steps.title: Signal the contradiction
steps.content: Choice A adds similar information, Choice B signals sequence, and Choice C signals a result. The needed phrase marks the contrary reality: no real hole existed. The answer is D.
